sentence_reviews.text: Compute-optimal training allocates a finite budget among parameters, tokens, steps, data quality, and hardware so expected performance is maximized.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Chinchilla supports compute-optimal allocation over parameters/tokens/compute, but data quality and hardware are broader than the cited formulation.

sentence_reviews.text: It avoids expensive undertraining or oversizing and makes scaling decisions explicit before a large run begins.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Chinchilla directly argues many large language models were undertrained and that compute-optimal allocation changes scaling decisions.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Chinchilla is directly about compute-optimal parameter-token allocation.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Budget-aware data mixtures are plausible but not directly supported by the Chinchilla/scaling-law citations.

sentence_reviews.text: Optimal ratios vary with data reuse, architecture, target tasks, objective, and deployment cost.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Scaling-law optima vary with modeling assumptions and objective; some listed factors are synthesized beyond the exact source claims.

sentence_reviews.text: Compute-optimal scaling & training allocation uses Empirical scaling laws as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Compute-optimal allocation is derived from empirical scaling-law fits.

sentence_reviews.text: Compute-optimal scaling & training allocation uses Data collection, curation & filtering as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Data quantity is central, but data curation/filtering as a core ingredient is broader than the cited source.

sentence_reviews.text: Compute-optimal scaling & training allocation uses Large-scale pretraining as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited work addresses large language model pretraining compute allocation.

sentence_reviews.text: Compute-optimal scaling & training allocation uses Distributed, mixed-precision & memory-efficient training as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Large runs use distributed systems in practice, but compute-optimal allocation does not inherently use mixed precision as a method ingredient.

edge_reviews.notes: Empirical scaling laws are the basis for compute-optimal training allocation.
Data budget matters, but curation/filtering is not directly established as core by the cited papers.
The sources concern language-model pretraining; common strength is reasonable.
Distributed/mixed precision is an implementation concern, not a directly supported ingredient of the allocation rule.
